golden_categories.name: Standard FQDN Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates fully qualified domain names (FQDN) under default settings.

golden_categories.name: Trailing Dot Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow trailing dots.

golden_categories.name: Non-TLD Validation
golden_categories.description: Handles FQDN validation when the requirement for a top-level domain (TLD) is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Numeric TLD Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with numeric TLDs when the requirement for a TLD is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Wildcard Domain Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.

golden_categories.name: Combined Options Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with multiple options enabled, including trailing dots, underscores, and numeric TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Default Validation
generated_categories.description: Covers the default behavior of the isFQDN validator without any options.

generated_categories.name: TLD Requirement
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where the requirement for a top-level domain (TLD) is toggled.

generated_categories.name: Underscore Handling
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where underscores are allowed in domain parts.

generated_categories.name: Trailing Dot Handling
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where trailing dots are allowed in domain names.

generated_categories.name: Numeric TLDs
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where numeric TLDs are allowed.

generated_categories.name: Wildcard Domains
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where wildcard domains are allowed.

generated_categories.name: Domain Length Limits
generated_categories.description: Covers scenarios where domain length limits are ignored.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories describe the default behavior of FQDN validation without any options.
Both categories focus on the validation of FQDNs with trailing dots allowed.
GC-3 describes scenarios where TLD requirement is disabled, while GENC-2 broadly covers toggling TLD requirements, including enabling or disabling it.
Both categories focus on validating FQDNs with numeric TLDs allowed.
Both categories focus on validating FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.
GC-6 describes combined options validation, including trailing dots, underscores, and numeric TLDs, which is not explicitly covered by any single generated category.

generated_to_golden.reason: Both categories describe the default behavior of FQDN validation without any options.
GENC-2 broadly covers toggling TLD requirements, while GC-3 specifically focuses on disabling the TLD requirement.
GENC-3 focuses on underscore handling, which is not explicitly covered by any golden category.
Both categories focus on the validation of FQDNs with trailing dots allowed.
Both categories focus on validating FQDNs with numeric TLDs allowed.
Both categories focus on validating FQDNs with wildcard domains enabled.
GENC-7 focuses on ignoring domain length limits, which is not explicitly covered by any golden category.

novel_generated_categories.reason: Underscore handling is a novel behavior not explicitly covered by any golden category.
Domain length limits are a novel behavior not explicitly covered by any golden category.

missing_golden_categories.reason: No generated categories achieved strong or partial semantic overlap with this fixed golden category.
